title: Introduction to Structs
description: Create your own custom data types.

slides.title: Beyond Built-in Types 🧬
slides.content:
## Building Concepts

`string`, `int`, and `bool` are great, but they are too simple.

How do you represent a "User" in Go? A user isn't just a string. A user has a Name (string), an Age (int), and an IsAdmin status (bool). 

We need to group these into a single custom type using a **Struct**.

quizData.question: What is a 'Struct' in Go?

options.text: A function that runs structured data
A collection of fields grouped together to form a custom data type
A tool for fixing code format

quizData.explanation: Structs (structures) let you bundle disparate data types into one cohesive custom type.

slides.title: Defining a Struct 📐
slides.content: You define a struct once at the top of your package, and then you can create as many copies of it as you want!

quizData.question: To access the `Age` field inside the `playerOne` struct variable, you type `playerOne_____Age`.
quizData.explanation: Go utilizes precise dot notation syntax (`.`) to accurately read internal struct fields actively.
